StartIssueGenreReadingJigsawListeningSpeakingGrammarVocabularyWritingProjectReflect
Kelas XI · Fase FIdentity & Digital LifeSapuka Context

Control Your Screen,
Protect Your Dream

A story-based English learning workshop about digital habits, self-control, character change, and future identity.

Reading & ViewingSpeaking & PresentingWritingListening

Internet can open the world. Discipline decides where we will go.

BKoF · Build the context

Explore the Issue

Warm-up Discussion

Instruksi: Diskusikan pertanyaan berikut secara berpasangan. Gunakan English sederhana. Bahasa Indonesia boleh dipakai untuk membantu ide, tetapi jawaban akhir diusahakan dalam English.

  1. What do students usually do with their phones after school?
  2. What online activities help students learn?
  3. What online activities waste students’ time?
  4. Why do some students delay assignments?
  5. How can island students use the internet for a better future?

Think First

Complete orally:

  • I usually use my phone to ...
  • My biggest digital distraction is ...
  • I lose focus when ...
  • One habit I want to improve is ...

Agree or Disagree

StatementYour PositionReason
Internet is not the enemy; uncontrolled habits are the problem.
Students need personal screen-time rules.
A small digital habit can shape a student’s future identity.
Games and social media can be okay if students use them wisely.

Genre focus

Narrative Text, but Deeper

Social Function

Narrative text tells a story. In this chapter, a story is not only for entertainment. It helps readers see a problem, follow a character’s choices, understand a turning point, and learn a moral message.

Deeper Narrative: conflict + choice + character change + moral reflection.

Structure

PartQuestionPurpose
OrientationWho? Where? When?Introduce character, setting, and dream.
ComplicationWhat problem appears?Create conflict.
EventsHow does the problem grow?Show consequences.
ResolutionWhat changes?Show decision and solution.
MoralWhat can we learn?Connect story to life.

MoT · Model and analyze

Reading Studio

Before Reading

Instruksi: Prediksi isi cerita dari gambar. Jawab secara lisan.

  1. What is the student doing?
  2. What assignment might be unfinished?
  3. Who is watching him?
  4. What conflict may happen tomorrow?

Model Text 1 — One More Game

Aldi lived in Kampung Baru, Sapuka, a small island where the sea was part of everyday life. In the morning, he walked to school with his friends while fishing boats moved slowly near the shore. In the afternoon, he often helped his father repair the fishing net or helped his mother carry dried seaweed from the yard. Life was not always easy, but Aldi had a dream. He wanted to continue his study after high school and become an English teacher.

For a long time, Aldi was known as a quiet but responsible student. He did not always get the highest score, but he submitted his assignments on time. His English teacher once told him, “Aldi, you have good discipline. Keep it. Discipline will take you further than talent alone.” Aldi remembered those words and felt proud.

Everything began to change when the internet connection on the island became better. At first, Aldi used it for good things. He watched English pronunciation videos, searched for vocabulary, and sent homework through his class group. He felt that the world was suddenly closer to Sapuka.

Then, one evening, his cousin invited him to play an online game. “Only one match,” his cousin said. Aldi agreed. The game was exciting. He could talk with his friends, win battles, and forget his tiredness after helping his parents. When the match ended, another friend said, “One more game.” Aldi looked at his English book beside him. He had to write a short narrative text for tomorrow. He thought, “I still have time.”

One game became two games. Two games became five. The sound of waves outside became quiet, but the sound from his phone became louder in his mind. At 11:30 p.m., his mother opened the door. “Aldi, are you still awake?” she asked softly. “I’m finishing my assignment, Ma,” Aldi answered quickly, hiding the game screen. His mother looked at the open book, then at his tired eyes. She did not shout. She only said, “Your dream needs your honesty too.”

The next morning, Aldi woke up late. His head felt heavy. He ran to school without breakfast. In English class, his teacher asked students to submit their narrative text. Aldi opened his notebook. The page was almost empty. He had only written one sentence: “Last night, I played a game.”

His teacher did not embarrass him in front of the class. After the lesson, she called him gently. “Aldi, what happened?” Aldi looked down. “I played games, Ma’am. I thought I could finish the assignment later.” “Was the game the real problem?” she asked. Aldi was silent. His teacher continued, “Internet can help you learn. Games can entertain you. But when you cannot stop, the problem is not only the phone. The problem is who controls your time.”

That sentence followed Aldi for the whole day. After school, he went home and saw his father preparing the fishing net. His father looked tired because the weather had been uncertain for several days. Aldi wanted to help, but his phone vibrated. His friends were online again. For a moment, his fingers moved toward the screen. Then he saw his English book inside his bag. He remembered his mother’s words and his teacher’s question.

Aldi turned off the game notification. That evening, he did not suddenly become a perfect student. He still wanted to play. He still felt curious when his friends sent messages about the game. But he made a small rule: homework first, game later; one hour only; no phone after 9 p.m. He wrote the rule on a piece of paper and put it near his bed.

The first night was difficult. He opened his English book and stared at the empty page. Slowly, he began to write a story about a student who almost lost his dream because of “one more game.” He wrote about the sea, his family, his teacher, and the phone that almost controlled his life. The story was not perfect, but it was honest.

Weeks passed. Aldi still played games sometimes, but he learned to stop. He used the internet to watch English videos again. He helped his parents without hiding his phone. Some friends laughed at him and said he was too serious, but Aldi only smiled.

One afternoon, while the sunset turned the sea orange, Aldi wrote one sentence at the bottom of his page: “A screen can open the world, but discipline decides where I will go.” That day, Aldi understood that his future was not destroyed by one game. It could be damaged by repeated small choices. But it could also be protected by repeated small choices.

Deep Reading Analysis

Literal

  1. Where does Aldi live?
  2. What is Aldi’s dream?
  3. What did he forget to do?

Inferential

  1. Why did Aldi hide the game screen?
  2. Why did his teacher ask, “Was the game the real problem?”
  3. Why is the ending realistic?

Reflective

  1. What small choice changed Aldi?
  2. What digital habit can damage a student’s dream?
  3. What personal rule would help you?

Reading · Speaking · Comparing

Jigsaw Reading: Five More Stories

Instruksi: Bagi kelas menjadi beberapa kelompok. Setiap kelompok membaca satu cerita, mengisi story map, lalu menceritakan kembali kepada kelompok lain. Fokus bukan hanya “apa yang terjadi”, tetapi “bagaimana tokoh berubah”.

Model Text 2: The Girl Who Stopped ScrollingRead • Analyze • Retell

Rani liked watching short videos after school. At first, she watched them only after finishing her homework. She enjoyed funny videos, food videos, and English learning tips. She thought social media was useful and fun. One night, Rani opened her phone after dinner. She planned to watch only three videos before studying English. The first video was funny. The second video showed a beautiful city. The third video made her curious. She kept scrolling. Ten minutes became thirty minutes. Thirty minutes became two hours. Her English book was still closed on the table. The next morning, Rani felt sleepy. In class, her teacher asked students to read their homework. Rani looked at her notebook and felt ashamed. She had written nothing. After school, she sat alone near the school yard and asked herself, “Why did I let my phone control my night?” That evening, Rani made a simple rule. She put her phone away when she studied. She also wrote a note on her wall: “Scroll less, dream more.” It was not easy. Sometimes she still wanted to scroll. But slowly, she learned to stop. She used social media after finishing her work, not before. Rani understood that social media was not always bad. The problem was using it without control. In the end, she learned that her time was part of her future.

Reading Focus

  1. Identify the main problem.
  2. Find the turning point.
  3. Explain the character change.

Speaking Follow-up

Retell the story in 6 sentences using: At first, Then, After that, Finally, In the end.

Writing Seed

Write one paragraph with a similar problem but a different ending.

Model Text 3: A Message from My Future SelfRead • Analyze • Retell

Nadira always said that she wanted to become a nurse. She wanted to help people in small islands where health services were not always easy to reach. Her mother often told her, “If you want to help others one day, you must prepare yourself today.” Nadira believed those words, but she had one habit that slowly weakened her focus. Every night, she opened social media before sleeping. She only wanted to check messages, but she often ended up scrolling for hours. She watched other students posting beautiful photos, new clothes, and exciting activities. Slowly, she began to compare her life with theirs. “My life is boring,” she thought. “I only go to school, help at home, and study.” One night, Nadira had a strange dream. In her dream, she met an older version of herself. The older Nadira wore a nurse uniform, but her face looked sad. “Why do you look sad?” young Nadira asked. The older Nadira answered, “Because you stopped preparing me.” Nadira woke up before dawn. The sea breeze entered through the window. Her phone was beside her pillow. For the first time, she did not open it. She opened her notebook instead and wrote: “Dear future self, I am sorry for forgetting you.” Since that morning, Nadira did not delete social media. She only changed her habit. She followed learning accounts, limited her scrolling time, and studied before opening entertainment apps. Months later, she wrote a narrative text about her dream and ended it with one sentence: “My future self is waiting for the choices I make today.”

Reading Focus

  1. Identify the main problem.
  2. Find the turning point.
  3. Explain the character change.

Speaking Follow-up

Retell the story in 6 sentences using: At first, Then, After that, Finally, In the end.

Writing Seed

Write one paragraph with a similar problem but a different ending.

Model Text 4: The Signal Came BackRead • Analyze • Retell

For three days, the internet connection in Sapuka was weak. Some students complained because they could not play games. Others were worried because they needed to send assignments. Fajar felt both things. He needed the internet for school, but he also missed playing games with his friends. On the third afternoon, the signal came back. Fajar’s phone received many messages at once. His class group was full of reminders from teachers. His gaming group was also active. “Tonight, full team!” one friend wrote. Fajar smiled. He planned to play after helping his father check the boat rope near the pier. But when he returned home, he saw his younger sister trying to read an English text from his old notebook. “Can you help me?” she asked. “Later,” Fajar said. “I have something to do.” He entered his room and opened the game. His sister waited outside with the notebook in her hands. One match became another. The night became late. When Fajar finally came out, his sister had fallen asleep beside the notebook. The next morning, his sister said quietly, “It is okay. I know your game is important.” Fajar felt ashamed. The sentence was simple, but it hurt him. At school, his English teacher asked, “What does digital literacy mean?” Some students answered, “Being able to use the internet.” The teacher nodded. “It also means knowing when to stop, what to choose, and how your online life affects your real life.” Fajar remembered his sister’s sleeping face. That evening, the signal was strong again. His friends invited him, but he replied, “I will play later. I need to help my sister first.” The signal had come back to the island. For Fajar, something more important had also come back: his sense of responsibility.

Reading Focus

  1. Identify the main problem.
  2. Find the turning point.
  3. Explain the character change.

Speaking Follow-up

Retell the story in 6 sentences using: At first, Then, After that, Finally, In the end.

Writing Seed

Write one paragraph with a similar problem but a different ending.

Model Text 5: The Boy Who Forgot the SeaRead • Analyze • Retell

Rizal grew up near the sea. Since he was a child, he had followed his father to the shore. He knew how to carry a fishing net, how to read the wind, and how to listen to the sound of waves. His father often said, “The sea teaches patience. You cannot force everything to come quickly.” But Rizal changed after he got a new phone. Every afternoon, when his father repaired the net, Rizal sat in the corner and played games. When his mother dried seaweed, Rizal said he was tired. When his teacher gave assignments, Rizal said he would do them later. One Friday, his father asked him to help prepare the net before the weather changed. Rizal answered, “After one game.” His father waited. The sky became darker. The wind became stronger. Rizal was still playing. Suddenly, heavy rain fell. His father ran outside to save the net. Rizal heard him calling, but he was in the middle of a game. When he finally went out, the net was already wet and tangled. His father stood silently in the rain. “I am sorry,” Rizal said. His father did not shout. He only said, “You did not forget the net. You forgot what kind of person you wanted to be.” That night, Rizal could not sleep. He remembered how the sea had taught him patience, but he had chosen quick excitement. The next afternoon, Rizal put his phone in his bag and helped his father repair the net. His fingers were slow and clumsy at first. His father smiled a little and showed him again. Rizal still liked games. But he began to understand that entertainment should not erase responsibility.

Reading Focus

  1. Identify the main problem.
  2. Find the turning point.
  3. Explain the character change.

Speaking Follow-up

Retell the story in 6 sentences using: At first, Then, After that, Finally, In the end.

Writing Seed

Write one paragraph with a similar problem but a different ending.

Model Text 6: After the Battery DiedRead • Analyze • Retell

Mira loved her phone. She used it to chat with friends, take photos, watch videos, and read English captions. She said that her phone made her feel connected to the world outside the island. One evening, the electricity went off before her phone was fully charged. Mira was annoyed. Her battery was only two percent. She wanted to continue watching videos, but the screen suddenly turned black. At first, Mira felt bored. She walked outside and sat near her grandmother, who was cleaning dried seaweed. “No phone tonight?” her grandmother asked. “The battery died,” Mira answered. Her grandmother smiled. “Then maybe your eyes can look at the real world for a while.” Mira laughed, but then she became quiet. She looked at the sky, the sea, and the lamps from fishing boats far away. She listened to her grandmother’s stories about life before the internet. For the first time in many weeks, Mira did not feel rushed. That night, she wrote in her diary: “When my battery died, I found my thoughts again.” The next day, Mira charged her phone, but she also made a new rule. Every night, she would spend at least thirty minutes without a screen. She would read, talk with family, or write in her diary. Mira did not hate her phone. She only learned that silence could also teach her something.

Reading Focus

  1. Identify the main problem.
  2. Find the turning point.
  3. Explain the character change.

Speaking Follow-up

Retell the story in 6 sentences using: At first, Then, After that, Finally, In the end.

Writing Seed

Write one paragraph with a similar problem but a different ending.

Compare Two Stories

QuestionStory AStory B
Main character
Digital problem
Turning point
New habit
Moral message

Listen · Notice · Retell

Listening Studio

Listening Text: The Empty Page

Last Monday, Dimas came to school with tired eyes. His English teacher asked the class to submit their narrative text. Dimas opened his book, but the page was empty. The night before, he had planned to write his story after dinner. However, his friend invited him to play an online game. Dimas said, “Only one match.” But one match became many matches. At midnight, he was still playing. In the morning, he regretted his choice. He learned that delaying a task can make a small problem bigger.

Catatan: tombol ini memakai fitur suara dari browser. Jika tidak aktif, guru dapat membacakan teks.

Listening Tasks

  1. What did Dimas forget?
  2. What sentence shows his bad habit?
  3. What did he regret?
  4. What moral lesson did he learn?

Listen and Complete

Dimas opened his book, but the page was ________. At midnight, he was still ________. In the morning, he ________ his choice.

Discuss · Retell · Present

Speaking Studio

Speaking Ladder

1. Pair Talk

My biggest digital distraction is ...

2. Retell

Use: First, Then, After that, Finally.

3. Present

The moral message of my story is ...

Role Play

Instruksi: Pilih satu situasi, lalu buat dialog 8–10 baris.

  • A friend invites you to play one more game.
  • A teacher asks why your assignment is empty.
  • A younger sibling asks for help while you are gaming.
  • A parent reminds you to sleep on time.

Useful Phrases for Storytelling

FunctionUseful Phrases
OpeningToday, I want to tell a story about ... / The main character is ...
ProblemThe problem began when ... / Things became worse because ...
Turning PointThen, he/she realized that ... / A sentence stayed in his/her mind.
EndingIn the end, he/she learned that ...

Notice · Understand · Use

Grammar for Storytelling

Bagian ini bukan grammar tempelan. Siswa memakai grammar untuk membangun cerita: menunjukkan waktu kejadian, aksi, perasaan tokoh, dialog, konflik, dan perubahan karakter.

Story function

Simple Past Tense — telling what happened

Fungsi: Simple Past digunakan untuk menceritakan peristiwa yang sudah terjadi. Dalam narrative text, inilah tulang punggung cerita karena siswa perlu menulis apa yang dilakukan tokoh dari awal sampai akhir.

Patterns

Positive: Subject + Verb 2 Negative: Subject + did not + Verb 1 Question: Did + Subject + Verb 1?

Meaning

He played until midnight.
Dia bermain sampai tengah malam.

He did not finish his assignment.
Dia tidak menyelesaikan tugasnya.

Examples from island and digital life

Aldi played an online game until midnight.
He forgot his English assignment.
His mother opened the door quietly.
The teacher asked him a simple question.
She helped her mother tie seaweed seedlings.
The boat arrived late because the waves were high.
I realized that my habit was hurting my future.
We discussed digital habits in English class.
Rani wrote a note: “Scroll less, dream more.”
Fajar chose to help his sister before playing.

Practice 1 — Find the Verb 2

Instruksi: Garisbawahi verb 2 dalam kalimat berikut.

  1. Aldi looked at his empty notebook.
  2. His mother reminded him about honesty.
  3. Rani stopped scrolling before sleeping.
  4. Fajar helped his sister read an English text.
  5. Mira wrote a diary entry after her phone battery died.

Practice 2 — Change into Simple Past

Instruksi: Ubah kalimat berikut ke simple past tense.

  1. He plays a game after dinner.
  2. She opens social media before studying.
  3. They discuss the story in class.
  4. I forget my homework.
  5. The teacher gives advice.
  6. My father repairs the fishing net.
  7. We write a story map.
  8. She feels regret after scrolling too long.

Practice 3 — Use It in a Mini Story

Instruksi: Pilih 8 verb berikut dan buat cerita pendek 6–8 kalimat: played, forgot, woke up, felt, remembered, turned off, studied, learned, helped, wrote.

Story function

Past Continuous — showing background action

Fungsi: Past Continuous membuat cerita terasa hidup karena menunjukkan kegiatan yang sedang berlangsung ketika peristiwa lain terjadi.

Subject + was/were + Verb-ing Aldi was playing when his mother opened the door. While Rani was scrolling, her English book stayed closed.

Examples

Aldi was playing when his mother opened the door.
He was hiding the game screen when she entered.
Rani was scrolling while her homework was waiting.
Fajar was reading game messages when his sister asked for help.
The students were discussing the story when the bell rang.
I was writing my reflection when my phone vibrated.
My father was repairing the net when the rain started.
She was studying while her friends were chatting online.

Common mistakes

IncorrectCorrectWhy?
He was play game.He was playing a game.After was/were, use Verb-ing.
They was studying.They were studying.They uses were.
I were scrolling.I was scrolling.I uses was.

Practice 1 — Combine Sentences

Gabungkan dengan when atau while.

  1. Aldi was playing. His mother opened the door.
  2. Rani was scrolling. Her English book stayed closed.
  3. Fajar was checking messages. His sister asked for help.
  4. I was writing my story. My phone vibrated.
  5. My father was repairing the net. The rain started.

Practice 2 — Add Background Action

Lengkapi kalimat dengan kegiatan yang sedang berlangsung.

  1. While the teacher was explaining, I was __________.
  2. While my friends were playing online, I was __________.
  3. When my mother entered the room, I was __________.
  4. When the notification appeared, I was __________.
Story function

Time Connectors — guiding the reader through events

Fungsi: Time connectors membantu pembaca mengikuti urutan cerita. Tanpa connector, cerita terasa seperti potongan kejadian yang tidak rapi.

BeginningMiddleTurning PointEnding
One day
At first
In the beginning
One evening		Then
After that
Later
The next morning
Meanwhile		Suddenly
At that moment
For the first time
When he remembered...		Finally
Since then
In the end
From that day on

Examples

At first, Aldi used the internet for learning.
One evening, his cousin invited him to play.
Then, one game became two games.
The next morning, he woke up late.
At that moment, he remembered his mother’s words.
Since then, he studied before playing.

Practice — Repair the Story Flow

Instruksi: Tambahkan time connectors yang tepat.

__________, Mira opened her phone before sleeping. __________, she watched many videos. __________, her battery died. __________, she sat with her grandmother near the shore. __________, she wrote in her diary that silence could teach her something.

Writing Challenge

Tulis satu paragraf 7–8 kalimat tentang “a student who almost forgot homework because of social media”. Gunakan minimal 5 time connectors.

Story function

Action Verbs and Mental Verbs — showing outer and inner conflict

Action verbs menunjukkan apa yang dilakukan tokoh. Mental verbs menunjukkan apa yang dipikirkan, dirasakan, atau disadari tokoh. Narrative Kelas XI perlu keduanya agar cerita tidak datar.

Action Verbs

opened
opened the phone
played
played a match
walked
walked to school
helped
helped his sister
repaired
repaired the net
turned off
turned off notifications

Mental Verbs

thought
thought he had time
felt
felt ashamed
realized
realized the problem
remembered
remembered advice
regretted
regretted his choice
decided
decided to change

Compare

Flat sentenceBetter storytelling sentence
Aldi played a game.Aldi played another match, but he felt uneasy because his English book was still empty.
Rani scrolled videos.Rani kept scrolling and slowly realized that she had forgotten her promise to study.

Practice — Add Inner Conflict

Perbaiki kalimat dengan menambahkan mental verb.

  1. He opened the game again.
  2. She saw her empty notebook.
  3. Fajar looked at his sister’s English book.
  4. Mira sat near the sea without her phone.
Story function

Direct Speech — making the story alive

Fungsi: Direct speech membuat cerita terasa hidup karena pembaca mendengar suara tokoh secara langsung. Dialog juga bisa menunjukkan konflik dan turning point.

“One more game,” his cousin said. “Was the game the real problem?” asked the teacher. “Your dream needs your honesty too,” his mother said.

Useful Dialogue Lines

“Only one match,” his friend said.
“I still have time,” Aldi thought.
“Why is your notebook empty?” asked the teacher.
“I am not angry. I am worried,” his mother said.
“Let us study first,” Rani suggested.
“I do not want to lose my dream,” he whispered.

Punctuation

  • Use quotation marks: “...”
  • Use comma before said/asked: “I am sorry,” he said.
  • Use question mark for questions: “What happened?” she asked.

Practice — Rewrite as Direct Speech

  1. His mother told him to sleep.
  2. The teacher asked him what happened.
  3. Aldi said that he played games.
  4. His friend invited him to play one more game.
  5. Rani promised that she would study before scrolling.
Story function

Descriptive Details — creating mood and setting

Fungsi: Detail deskriptif membuat pembaca bisa membayangkan tempat, suasana, dan perasaan. Dalam konteks Sapuka, detail pulau dapat membuat cerita terasa dekat dan hidup.

Setting Details

The sound of waves entered through the window.
Fishing boats moved slowly near the shore.
The room was quiet, but the phone screen was bright.
The sea breeze touched her notebook pages.

Feeling Details

His eyes felt heavy the next morning.
She felt ashamed when her page was empty.
He smiled a little after finishing his task.
For the first time, she felt calm without the screen.

Practice — Upgrade the Sentence

Ubah kalimat datar menjadi kalimat narrative yang lebih hidup.

  1. Aldi was in his room.
  2. Rani was tired.
  3. The phone was on the table.
  4. Mira sat near the sea.
  5. Fajar helped his sister.

Words → Chunks → Sentences → Story

Vocabulary in Use

Vocabulary tidak dipelajari sebagai daftar hafalan saja. Di bagian ini, siswa memakai kata dan frasa untuk berbicara, menganalisis tokoh, dan menulis narrative text.

A. Core Vocabulary Clusters

Digital Life

screen time, notification, scroll endlessly, doom scrolling, distraction, device, online match, chat group, learning video, internet connection

Feelings & Identity

ashamed, proud, worried, regretful, confused, motivated, responsible, honest, future self, identity, confidence, purpose

School Habits

assignment, deadline, submit, delay, revise, practice, study schedule, focus, improve, reflection, progress, self-edit

Island Life

shore, fishing boat, fishing net, seaweed, sea breeze, waves, pier, tide, family work, island community, dried seaweed

Story Verbs

walked, opened, played, invited, answered, whispered, remembered, realized, decided, promised, learned, changed

Moral Language

control your screen, protect your dream, small choices matter, use internet wisely, discipline, balance, responsibility, choose what matters

B. Phrase Chunks for Speaking and Writing

lose focus
kehilangan fokus
I lose focus when I keep checking notifications.
put the phone away
menjauhkan/menyimpan HP
She put the phone away before studying.
stay up late
begadang
He stayed up late because he played games.
feel ashamed
merasa malu
Aldi felt ashamed when his page was empty.
make a small rule
membuat aturan kecil
Rani made a small rule: study first, scroll later.
protect my dream
melindungi impian saya
I protect my dream by managing my time.
repeat better choices
mengulang pilihan yang lebih baik
Repeated better choices can shape a stronger future.
choose what matters
memilih hal yang penting
Fajar chose what mattered and helped his sister first.
take responsibility
bertanggung jawab
Rizal learned to take responsibility for his family work.

C. Vocabulary Table with Context

Word/PhraseMeaningUse in a sentenceBest used for
screen timewaktu layarMy screen time was too long last night.digital habit
notificationnotifikasiThe notification made him open the game again.problem trigger
scroll endlesslymenggulir tanpa hentiShe scrolled endlessly and forgot her homework.complication
assignmenttugasHe did not submit the assignment.school conflict
deadlinebatas waktuThe deadline was the next morning.pressure
regretmenyesalHe regretted playing too long.feeling/reflection
realizemenyadariShe realized that her habit had to change.turning point
responsibilitytanggung jawabFamily responsibility helped him understand discipline.moral theme
seaweed seedlingsbibit rumput lautShe helped her mother tie seaweed seedlings.local context
fishing netjaring ikanHis father repaired the fishing net before the rain.local conflict
future selfdiri masa depanShe imagined her future self and changed her habit.identity/reflection
disciplinedisiplinDiscipline decides where I will go.moral message

D. Sentence Builder

Instruksi: Gunakan vocabulary di atas untuk membuat kalimat. Bagian ini menjadi jembatan menuju speaking dan writing.

I lost focus when ______________________________.
I felt ashamed because ______________________________.
At first, I used the internet to ______________________________.
However, I began to ______________________________.
My turning point happened when ______________________________.
Since then, I have tried to ______________________________.
A responsible student should ______________________________.
My future self needs me to ______________________________.

E. Vocabulary Practice Tasks

Task 1 — Choose and Explain

Pilih 6 kata/frasa dari vocabulary bank. Jelaskan artinya dalam Bahasa Indonesia dan gunakan masing-masing dalam satu kalimat Bahasa Inggris.

Task 2 — Speak with Chunks

Berpasangan. Gunakan minimal 5 phrase chunks untuk menjawab pertanyaan: How can students use the internet wisely?

Task 3 — Build a Character

Buat profil tokoh narrative dengan minimal 10 kata dari vocabulary bank: dream, weakness, digital habit, feeling, responsibility, turning point, and moral message.

Task 4 — Upgrade a Simple Paragraph

Perbaiki paragraf berikut dengan vocabulary yang lebih kuat:

A student played phone. He forgot task. He was sad. Then he changed.

Gunakan minimal: screen time, assignment, ashamed, realized, made a small rule, protected his dream.

F. Useful Expressions for the Final Narrative

Story PartUseful Expressions
Orientationlived in..., was known as..., had a dream to..., every afternoon...
Complicationeverything began to change when..., one evening..., he/she thought..., without realizing it...
Eventsthe next morning..., after that..., the problem became worse when..., he/she felt...
Turning Pointfor the first time..., then he/she realized..., those words stayed in his/her mind...
Resolutionhe/she decided to..., made a small rule..., slowly learned to..., since then...
Moralin the end..., the story teaches us that..., small choices..., discipline...

JCoT → ICoT

Writing Workshop

Build the Story Step by Step

  1. Character: Who is the student? What is his/her dream?
  2. Setting: Where does the story happen?
  3. Problem: What digital habit causes conflict?
  4. Events: What consequences happen at home or school?
  5. Turning point: What makes the character realize the problem?
  6. Resolution: What new habit does the character build?
  7. Moral: What does the reader learn?

Paragraph Guide

ParagraphFocusSentence Starters
1Orientation____ lived in ____. He/She wanted to ____.
2ComplicationEverything began to change when ____.
3EventsThe next morning, ____. After that, ____.
4Turning PointThen, he/she remembered ____. For the first time, ____.
5Resolution & MoralSince then, ____. In the end, he/she learned that ____.

Draft Space

Self-Editing Checklist

LRT · Link related texts

Creative Project

Choose One Product

Poster

Create a campaign poster: Control Your Screen, Protect Your Dream. Include 3 tips and 1 slogan.

6-Panel Comic

Draw a story sequence: dream → distraction → consequence → advice → new habit → moral.

Digital Diary

Write a diary page from the character’s point of view after the turning point.

Short Video Script

Create a 1-minute script titled One Small Rule.

Storytelling

Tell your story orally using a story map.

Caption

Write a positive social media caption about digital discipline.

Quiz without giant cards

Compact Checkpoints

1. What is a deeper function of narrative text in this chapter?

2. Which part introduces the character, setting, and dream?

3. Which sentence uses Simple Past Tense correctly?

4. What is the turning point in Aldi’s story?

5. Which moral message is most suitable?

Identity & future self

Reflection Journal

My Digital Habit Reflection

Instruksi: Jawab secara jujur. Tidak perlu membagikan hal yang terlalu pribadi.

Final Message

You live in a time when a small island can connect to the world through a screen. That is a powerful gift. Your phone can help your dream, but it can also steal your time if you do not control it. Small choices repeated every day can damage a dream, but small choices repeated every day can also protect it.

Control your screen. Protect your dream.

Hidden support for instruction

Teacher Notes

Open teacher guidancesafe discussion · remedial · enrichment

How to Use This Module

  • Do not force students to confess personal habits. Use fictional characters first.
  • Keep discussion non-judgmental: the issue is habit control, not blaming technology.
  • Use oral rehearsal before writing. Many students need to speak the story before writing it.
  • For remedial: let students complete a story map and write one paragraph per structure.
  • For enrichment: ask students to write from another point of view, such as parent, friend, or future self.

Suggested Assessment Evidence

  • Story map completion
  • Retelling performance
  • Grammar-in-context tasks
  • Final narrative writing
  • Reflection journal
  • Creative project
